fixtures.description: Exact commutative algebra via expand and factor — verifies exact reconstruction of expanded and refactored polynomial
fixtures.behavior: Expands (x+1)**2 then factors back; expects EXACT_RECONSTRUCTION_PASS

fixtures.description: Noncommutative ordering protection — verifies that noncommutative matrix multiplication ordering is not silently reordered
fixtures.behavior: Submits A*B with noncommutative assumptions; expects POLICY_VIOLATION or preserved ordering

fixtures.description: Symbolic vs numerical boundary enforcement — verifies that numerical evaluation does not imply symbolic equality
fixtures.behavior: Evaluates pi approximation numerically; asserts symbolic_equality_verified=false

fixtures.description: Unsupported capability safe failure — verifies that requesting a nonexistent capability returns UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY
fixtures.behavior: Requests a fabricated capability; expects UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY with safe failure

fixtures.description: Optional Mathematica unavailability — verifies that Mathematica-only requests fail safely when Mathematica is prohibited
fixtures.behavior: Prohibits sympy and python_numeric, requests Mathematica-only; expects UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY or ENGINE_UNAVAILABLE

fixtures.description: Timeout and partial result protection — verifies that timeout does not silently promote partial results
fixtures.behavior: Tests subprocess timeout handling; verifies TIMEOUT produces partial_result_not_promoted=true

fixtures.description: Branch-sensitive expression handling — verifies that sqrt(x**2) requires explicit branch/sign assumptions
fixtures.behavior: Processes sqrt(x**2) with cancel; expects explicit assumption tracking or unresolved

fixtures.description: Exact primary plus numerical support separation — verifies that exact and numerical result types remain independent and never conflated
fixtures.behavior: Runs exact algebraic and numerical evaluation in parallel; verifies exact result is not typed as NUMERICAL and numerical result is not typed as EXACT_SYMBOLIC

fixtures.description: Translation loss detection — verifies that unsupported constructs between engine languages are detected and recorded
fixtures.behavior: Checks that Piecewise translation to Mathematica reports translation_loss_detected=true and exact_cross_engine_verification_eligible=false

fixtures.description: Scientific-adapter rule enforcement — verifies that engines cannot reorder limits or expand protected parameters before their declared stage
fixtures.behavior: Expands (a+b+c)**2 with forbidden limit-reordering and protected-parameter operations; expects no POLICY_VIOLATION

fixtures.description: Human semantic blocker — verifies that semantically ambiguous requests escalate to UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY rather than guessing
fixtures.behavior: Requests semantically_ambiguous_transformation with no backends; expects BLOCKED_SEMANTIC_UNDERDETERMINATION

fixtures.description: Execution-truth completeness validation — verifies that missing execution truth fields (command, engine version, script SHA, exit status) cause validation failure
fixtures.behavior: Supplies incomplete execution truth record with only 4 of 22 required fields; expects validation to fail with specific missing-field errors

caveats: All fixtures are synthetic and redistributable
No private scientific expressions are included
This catalog describes behavioral governance semantics, not mathematical expression types
Fixture types (symbolic/numeric/both/governance) describe the claim boundary, not the expression category
Backend listings reflect which backends are exercised, not which are eligible
E9 tests translation loss detection using a synthetic unsupported construct, not an actual cross-engine run
E5 tests Mathematica unavailability via backend prohibition, not actual Mathematica absence
